$comment: Boundary-case fixture for the distributed-boundary matrix. Not a live release packet. See docs/compat/boundary_matrix.md.
scenario: A profile-layout producer emits an additive field (a remembered-layout detail) ahead of a consumer that was built against the prior additive frontier. Both sides sit in schema epoch 1 and the consumer preserves unknown additive fields. The capability intersection is the full advertised set; the producer leads within the additive frontier, the consumer continues at its prior frontier without breaking state.

supported_skew_window.summary: Producer and consumer preserve one schema epoch with additive-field tolerance; unknown additive fields are preserved, required-field or meaning-changing drift is out of window.

upgrade_order.notes: Producers may ship additive fields ahead of consumers provided consumers preserve unknown additive fields. Breaking epoch moves require consumer upgrades first.

rollback_order.notes: Roll the producer back to the prior additive frontier before rolling the consumer back so the consumer is never asked to parse a future-only field during rollback.

unsupported_state_behavior.contract_rule: Consumers that cannot attribute a required field refuse the payload with an attributed error. Silent truncation is forbidden. Explicitly excluded or admin-owned state rendered as portable user truth fails closed.

what_happens_when_upgrade_order_violated: If the consumer is promoted past the producer across an epoch boundary (consumer at epoch 2 reading producer epoch 1), the boundary refuses ingest with an attributed error rather than guessing meanings for renamed or repurposed fields. Cross-epoch downgrade is a separate migration packet.
what_happens_when_skew_window_exceeded: If the payload carries a required field the consumer cannot attribute, the consumer refuses with an attributed error. There is no silent truncation or best-guess rewrite. Unknown additive fields are preserved round-trip so the producer's frontier remains intact.
notes: Illustrative seed boundary case; this is not a live release packet. Also exemplifies boundary_row:audit_or_event_producer_consumer and boundary_row:cli_or_export_boundary which share the same additive-epoch contract.
